signal_class_brief:
Different participant pools dominate at different times of day and week, creating structural microstructure effects that are reducible — they exist because of who is trading, not because of chart patterns.

Session windows (UTC): Asian session roughly 00:00–08:00, European session 07:00–16:00, US session 13:00–21:00. The Asian session is dominated by retail crypto participants; European and US sessions have more institutional flow and deeper liquidity. Directional moves that initiate in the Asian session (low liquidity, retail-driven) frequently exhaust or reverse at the US open. Conversely, moves that begin in the US session with volume often sustain through the next day.

Weekend effects: Saturday and Sunday trading is predominantly retail. Volume is lower, and momentum signals are less reliable. A filter that avoids new entries on weekends (df.index.dayofweek >= 5) has an empirical basis in participant structure.

Hour-of-day effects: The 13:00–15:00 UTC window (US market open + first hour) historically exhibits the highest BTC volatility and volume as institutional desks come online. The 02:00–06:00 UTC window (deep Asia/Australia, low global participation) tends to see slow drift or mean-reversion.
reducibility_note: Moderate reducibility. Session effects exist because of structurally different participant pools at different times — this is a microstructure property, not a reflexive chart pattern. However, as more participants become aware of these effects, they can partially arbitrage away. They are more durable than TA but less robust than pure volatility clustering.
exploration_hints: Hour-of-day from UTC index: `df.index.hour` — available directly on the DatetimeIndex.
US session entry filter: only enter between 13:00–20:00 UTC (`(df.index.hour >= 13) & (df.index.hour < 20)`).
Asian session avoidance: block new entries between 01:00–07:00 UTC when retail flow dominates.
Session momentum: price change in first 2 hours of US session as directional signal for the day.
Try combining a simple momentum entry (close > close.shift(N)) with a session timing gate instead of EMA crossover.
End-of-week exit: close all positions before weekend (e.g. exit on Friday 20:00 UTC).
Day-of-week weighting: track which weekdays have the best win rate historically and use as an entry gate.
